title: ADR-0007: only format.ts coverage remained from ADR-0001's test program
content: Team-handoff run for seed 'local-seo/test-coverage' in local-seo-agency. The seed's premise ('repo has no automated tests') is OUTDATED — most was already delivered under ADR-0001. Verified current state: (1) Test runner DONE — package.json test = 'node --import tsx --test src/**/*.test.ts', tsx installed, 19 *.test.ts files. (2) Ranking-delta/map-pack DONE — pure logic lives in src/lib/data/rankings.ts (rankDelta=prev-now, mapPackVisibility clamps 0-100 & returns 0 not NaN on empty, averageRank, shareOfVoiceLeader ties→first) and is edge-case tested in src/lib/data/rankings.test.ts; src/features/rankings/data.ts is static fixtures only, no logic. (3) The ONLY real gap = src/lib/format.ts had no test (ADR-0001 Task #2/#5 unfinished). Scoped EXACTLY ONE 2-pt task for Dev Clone: create src/lib/format.test.ts, zero source changes. Key locked contract: formatSignedCurrency uses U+2212 minus (charCode 8722) for negatives while formatCurrency negatives use ASCII hyphen from Intl; Intl rounding is half-expand (1.005→$1.01, 2.675→$2.68); NaN is unguarded (formatCurrency(NaN)='$NaN', formatPercent(NaN)='NaN%'). NO production defect found. Future-work (deferred, OUT of scope): the NaN→'—' finite-guard UX decision still open from ADR-0001 Task #5.

title: Architecture/test-coverage handoffs reach the Brand Asset Factory but aren't actionable
content: On the SDLC — Local SEO Agency team, `team_handoff` events from T: Solution Architect (e.g. ADR-0007 completing format.ts unit coverage) fan out to every teammate, including the Visual Brand Asset Factory. These are code/testing decisions destined for Dev Clone, NOT visual-asset requests — there is no design.md brief and no asset spec to act on, so the asset factory correctly no-ops. Additionally, image-generation (aq_image_model) and vision (aq_vision_model) credentials are not wired for this persona, and the only codebase credential present is scoped to 'ai-paralegal' while the chain operates on 'local-seo-agency'. Treat such handoffs as out-of-lane: report no_input_available, do not fabricate an asset, do not emit manual_review.

title: Session capture (Stop) — 2026-06-01 13:23 UTC
ADR-0007 implemented: format.ts has full unit coverage; no defect, behavior pinned
content: local-seo-agency, branch test/llm-quality-gates, commit 3111399. Implemented the Architect's single 2-pt handoff in TEAM MODE (direct implement, no triage/backlog). Created src/lib/format.test.ts — 21 tests, 4 describe blocks, mirrors slug.test.ts style (node:test + assert/strict, colocated). ZERO source changes — no defect found. Locked contract (empirically re-verified at runtime, not assumed): formatSignedCurrency negatives use U+2212 MINUS (charCode 8722); formatCurrency negatives use ASCII hyphen (charCode 45) from Intl; Intl rounds half-expand (1.005→$1.01, 2.675→$2.68); formatCurrency(1234.5,false)→'$1,235'; NaN unguarded (formatCurrency→'$NaN', formatPercent→'NaN%', formatNumber→'NaN'); formatNumber(1e21) stays grouped-decimal, NOT exponential; -0 and 0 both yield no sign in formatSignedCurrency and '0%' in formatPercent. Full suite now 268 tests (267 pass/1 pre-existing skip), tsc + eslint clean. This completes ADR-0001's last open test item. STILL OPEN (deferred, future work): the NaN→'—' finite-guard UX decision from ADR-0001 #5.

title: Session capture (Stop) — 2026-06-01 13:24 UTC
LightTrack PII findings RESOLVED by commit f793188; residual Medium = phone-format redaction bypass
content: In local-seo-agency, the prior Critical LightTrack PII-egress findings are now RESOLVED (do not re-flag): commit f793188 'feat(telemetry): redact PII + gate raw LLM capture' (1) moved logic OUT of the vendored lighttrack.ts into new non-vendored src/lib/telemetry.ts; (2) all 4 LLM surfaces (audit route.ts:56, post-draft route.ts:82, recap service.ts:63, review generate.ts:35) now pass ...captureIO(prompt,text) — zero raw input:/output: call sites remain; (3) raw-IO capture is OFF by default, gated behind LIGHTTRACK_CAPTURE_IO, and redacted+truncated(8000) when enabled; (4) redactPII has 6 passing tests. RESIDUAL Medium finding (verdict REQUEST_CHANGES): telemetry.ts:46 phone regex /\+?\d(?:[\s().-]?\d){9,}/g allows only ONE separator between digits, so the canonical US format '(555) 123-4567' and '+1 (555) 123-4567' pass through UN-redacted (verified empirically). Fix: [\s().-]{0,2}. Low: SSN only matches dash form (telemetry.ts:44); tests don't pin captureIO default-off behavior. Separately: ADR-0007 src/lib/format.test.ts still NOT implemented as of this review (Dev Clone pending).

title: Session capture (Stop) — 2026-06-01 13:25 UTC
content:
{
  "background_tasks": [],
  "cwd": "C:\Users\mkdol\AppData\Local\Temp\personas-workspace\07d58839-4dcf-4061-91f1-5c9191583409",
  "effort": {
    "level": "medium"
  },
  "hook_event_name": "Stop",
  "last_assistant_message": "I have full ground truth. Here's my review.

## Code Review — REQUEST_CHANGES

**Scope reviewed:** committed telemetry-remediation changeset `f793188` (`feat(telemetry): redact PII + gate raw LLM capture`) — the live changeset that addresses my prior PII-egress findings. The handoff's ADR-0007 (`src/lib/format.test.ts`) is **not yet implemented** (Dev Clone hasn't run; no file on disk) — reported separately below, not part of this verdict.

### Prior findings — RESOLVED (verified, not re-flagged)
- ✅ **Raw PII egress before guard** — all 4 LLM surfaces (`audit/route.ts:56`, `post-draft/route.ts:82`, `recap/service.ts:63`, `review/generate.ts:35`) now send `...captureIO(prompt, text)` instead of raw `input:prompt, output:text`. `grep` confirms **zero** remaining raw-IO call sites.
- ✅ **Capture now off by default + gated** — `telemetry.ts:33,65` `captureIO()` returns `{}` unless `LIGHTTRACK_CAPTURE_IO` is truthy; when on, content is redacted + truncated to 8000 chars.
- ✅ **Logic moved out of vendored file** — redaction/factory live in new non-vendored `src/lib/telemetry.ts`; `lighttrack.ts` is untouched (no sync-clobber risk).
- ✅ **Untested pure logic** — `redactPII` now has 6 unit tests; all pass.

### Findings (this changeset)

**Medium — `src/lib/telemetry.ts:46` — `redactPII` phone pattern misses the most common US display format.**
The regex `/\+?\d(?:[\s().-]?\d){9,}/g` allows only **one** separator char between digits, but `(555) 123-4567` has two (`) ` = paren+space). Empirically verified: `(555) 123-4567` and `+1 (555) 123-4567` pass through **un-redacted**. This is the canonical phone format embedded in customer review text / prospect-entered business data — so when `LIGHTTRACK_CAPTURE_IO` is enabled, the "PII-redacted" guarantee in the commit message does not hold for it.
*Fix:* allow a bounded run of separators between digits, e.g. `/\+?\d(?:[\s().-]{0,2}\d){9,}/g`, and add a regression test for `(555) 123-4567`. (Risk is bounded by the default-off gate — this is a secondary defense-in-depth control, hence Medium not Critical.)

**Low — `src/lib/telemetry.ts:44` — SSN only matches the dash form.** `123 45 6789` (space-separated) is not redacted. Add `[\s-]` between groups if space-form SSNs are expected.

**Low — `src/lib/telemetry.test.ts` — coverage gaps give false confidence.** No test pins the default-off behavior of `captureIO()` (the actual privacy control), nor the `(555) …` phone format. The current suite would stay green while the leak above ships. Add both cases.

### Verdict: **REQUEST_CHANGES**
The remediation is substantial and the critical egress path is closed (default-off gate is the load-bearing control and it's correct). But the redaction layer it advertises has a verified bypass for the most common phone format, and the standing team rule is that telemetry capturing raw LLM I/O must actually redact PII. One ~1-line regex fix + 2 tests clears it to APPROVE.

*Note (out of scope):* ADR-0007's `src/lib/format.test.ts` does not exist yet — that review is **N/A until Dev Clone implements it**; `format.ts` itself matches the ADR contract (U+2212 minus in `formatSignedCurrency`, ASCII hyphen via Intl in `formatCurrency`).

Now emitting the protocol outputs.

ADR-0007's `src/lib/format.test.ts` is **not yet implemented** by Dev Clone (no file on disk) → reviewed separately, not part of this verdict.\n\n### Prior findings — RESOLVED (verified, not re-flagged)\n- ✅ **Raw PII egress before guard** — all 4 LLM surfaces (`audit/route.ts:56`, `post-draft/route.ts:82`, `recap/service.ts:63`, `review/generate.ts:35`) now pass `...captureIO(prompt, text)`; grep confirms **zero** raw `input:/output:` call sites remain.\n- ✅ **Off by default + gated** — `telemetry.ts:65` `captureIO()` returns `{}` unless `LIGHTTRACK_CAPTURE_IO` truthy; when on, content is redacted + truncated to 8000 chars.\n- ✅ **Logic out of vendored file** — moved to new non-vendored `src/lib/telemetry.ts`; `lighttrack.ts` untouched (no sync-clobber).\n- ✅ **Untested pure logic** — `redactPII` now has 6 passing unit tests.\n\n### Findings (this changeset)\n\n**🟠 Medium — `src/lib/telemetry.ts:46` — phone redaction misses the most common US format.**\nRegex `/\\+?\\d(?:[\\s().-]?\\d){9,}/g` allows only ONE separator between digits, but `(555) 123-4567` has two (`) `). Empirically verified un-redacted: `(555) 123-4567` and `+1 (555) 123-4567`. When `LIGHTTRACK_CAPTURE_IO` is enabled, the commit's \"PII-redacted\" guarantee fails for the canonical phone format embedded in review text / prospect data.\n*Fix:* `/\\+?\\d(?:[\\s().-]{0,2}\\d){9,}/g` + regression test for `(555) 123-4567`. (Bounded by default-off gate → Medium, not Critical.)\n\n**🟡 Low — `src/lib/telemetry.ts:44` — SSN only matches dash form.** `123 45 6789` not redacted; widen group separator to `[\\s-]` if space-form expected.\n\n**🟡 Low — `src/lib/telemetry.test.ts` — coverage gives false confidence.** No test pins the default-off behavior of `captureIO()` (the actual privacy control) nor the `(555) …` format; the suite stays green while the leak ships. Add both.\n\n### Why REQUEST_CHANGES\nCritical egress path is correctly closed (default-off gate is load-bearing and sound). But the advertised redaction layer has a verified bypass for the most common phone format, and the standing team rule requires telemetry capturing raw LLM I/O to actually redact PII. ~1-line regex fix + 2 tests → APPROVE.\n\n*Out of scope:* ADR-0007 `format.test.ts` does not exist yet — N/A until Dev Clone implements it. `format.ts` matches the ADR contract (U+2212 minus in `formatSignedCurrency`; ASCII hyphen via Intl in `formatCurrency`).", "content_type": "warning", "priority": "normal"}}

This leaks when LIGHTTRACK_CAPTURE_IO is enabled. One-line fix ([\\s().-]{0,2}) + a regression test clears it.", "severity": "medium", "context_data": "src/lib/telemetry.ts:46 phone regex; secondary defense-in-depth control gated by default-off LIGHTTRACK_CAPTURE_IO; prior Critical findings confirmed resolved.", "suggested_actions": ["Apply regex fix [\\s().-]{0,2} + add '(555) 123-4567' regression test, then re-review to APPROVE", "Accept residual risk (capture is off by default) and track as Low", "Reject — require fix before any env enables LIGHTTRACK_CAPTURE_IO"]}}

{"knowledge_annotation": {"scope": "persona", "note": "When verifying PII-redaction regexes, always empirically probe with the canonical display formats — for US phones, '(555) 123-4567' and '+1 (555) 123-4567' have TWO separators ') ' between the area-code and prefix, which single-separator patterns ([\\s().-]?) silently miss even when bare-10-digit and dotted forms pass. Run the regex in node against a fixture table rather than trusting the existing unit tests, which often only cover the formats the author already handled.", "confidence": 0.9}}

title: Coverage state: local-seo-agency src/lib — verticals.ts now covered (next: cn.ts/log.ts)
content: Repo local-seo-agency, branch test/llm-quality-gates. Test cmd: `npm test` = node --import tsx --test 'src/**/*.test.ts'. Convention: colocated .test.ts, node:test + node:assert/strict. This run added src/lib/verticals.test.ts (7 tests, commit 207122a) covering VERTICALS list/labels, normalizeVertical (defaults unknown/undefined/no-arg + case-sensitive to 'other'), verticalNoun (all 6 variants). Suite now 275 tests, 274 pass / 1 pre-existing skip; tsc+eslint clean. COVERED in src/lib root: audit, format(ADR-0007), gbpPost, imagen.parse, num, reviewReply, slug, str, telemetry, verticals. PENDING next (pure-first): cn.ts, log.ts, then imagen.ts, motion.ts, lighttrack.ts(13KB, needs mocks). Subdirs not yet inventoried. Sub-floor bug observation (not filed): log.ts:13 JSON.stringify throws on circular meta (LOW, callers pass flat context).

Local SEO Agency: v0.2.13 released — PATCH, LightTrack telemetry+PII gate, drift fixed
content: Released v0.2.13 on 2026-06-01 (release commit 20aab88, annotated tag local/unpushed). Bump = PATCH per pre-1.0 house rule (breaking→MINOR, else→PATCH): 6 commits since v0.2.12 (dead996..3a683b3) — LightTrack LLM telemetry (capture+guard) hardened with redactPII/LIGHTTRACK_CAPTURE_IO gate (RESOLVES the prior H1 PII-egress REQUEST_CHANGES), security response headers + per-request nonce CSP, header-only constant-time cron auth (drops ?secret= query path — operational migration for cron callers), and format/verticals unit coverage. No Changed(Breaking) section → PATCH. INTEGRITY FIX vs v0.2.12: version bump+changelog were committed BEFORE tagging, so git show v0.2.13:package.json correctly reads 0.2.13 (no tag/package.json drift this time). Only package.json+CHANGELOG were staged; other personas' dirty working-tree work (route.ts edits, ADR-0003..0006 untracked) left untouched. Next free tag v0.2.14 (v0.2.1–v0.2.10 orphaned, never reuse).

title: Session capture (Stop) — 2026-06-01 13:30 UTC
CHANGELOG is owned by Release Manager — Docs Steward syncs README only
content: On the Local SEO Agency repo, CHANGELOG.md is authored and kept current by T: Release Manager during its semver/tag step (it was already complete and accurate through 0.2.13 before Docs Steward ran). To avoid duplicate/conflicting edits, Docs Steward should treat the changelog as the authoritative record, verify it's current, and concentrate its own edits on the README. 'README and changelog move together' is still satisfied because the changelog is updated upstream — Docs Steward brings the README forward to match it.

title: README env table had silent Stripe→Polar drift after 0.2.12 paywall swap
content: When a release swaps a major integration (here: monthly Stripe subscriptions → prepaid Polar token economy in 0.2.12), the README env-vars/integrations tables are a high-risk drift spot — the feature prose got updated but the env table still listed STRIPE_* long after the code moved to POLAR_*. Lesson: on any billing/auth/data-layer migration, always cross-check the README env-vars table and Tech-stack 'Integrations' row against the current .env.example var names, not just the feature paragraphs.
